issues.criterion: F-010/AT acceptance-criterion #3: 'tools/SKILL.md remains a router rather than a mirror'. Revision 1's fix (new entry 13, structural per-bullet anti-paraphrase) is scoped ONLY to the four lines matching the bullet prefix '- `<path>`'; it does not constrain any other line in the new Section 8.
issues.evidence: On a disposable full-repository clone at the real, current HEAD 3aed5d1 (039 now committed): built an honest, fully-compliant Section 8 (four one-line bullets, each 'Preflight'/'Baseline'/'Audit'/'Compare' label -- 16 net added lines), then appended ONE additional prose paragraph immediately after the four bullets (not matching any bullet prefix, so entirely outside entry 13's inspection) restating all four protocols' core operative rules in ordinary sentences (verdict-enum + operator-sign-off gate; single-revision-only baseline rule; confidence-bounded-by-evidence rule; per-finding traceability rule) without reusing any of the four exact canary sentences. Net result: `git diff --numstat HEAD -- tools/SKILL.md` = 18 lines added. Ran entry 13 (structural per-bullet check): PASS (the four bullets themselves are untouched and structurally sound -- the check never inspects the added paragraph at all). Ran entry 15 (verbatim-canary): PASS (no exact canary sentence reused). Ran entry 16 (40-line cap): PASS (18<=40). Ran entry 12 (content-completeness): PASS. This is materially the SAME substantive-mirror content that triggered the original round-0 HIGH finding, now delivered as a same-section prose paragraph instead of bullet-continuation text, at fewer lines (18) than even the original round-0 fixture (25) -- and it defeats every one of the four legs of the router-vs-mirror proof simultaneously, including the new one. Revision 1's structural fix closes the two specific attack shapes it was fixture-tested against (multi-line bullet stretch; over-length/over-word bullet label) but does not close, and was not tested against, the much simpler 'add a free-standing paragraph elsewhere in the same section' attack -- which remains exactly as game-able as before revision 1.
issues.required_fix: Widen entry 13 (or add a companion entry) so the WHOLE of the new Section 8's content is a closed set: assert that every non-blank line between the '## 8. Ecosystem Engineering Cycle Routing' heading and the next '## ' heading is EITHER (a) one of a small, explicitly enumerated set of preamble sentences/lines the contract itself pins verbatim (design_notes already effectively commits to specific preamble wording -- make that commitment a checked invariant, e.g. exact-line-count or exact-text match for the preamble), OR (b) one of the four structurally-validated bullets, OR (c) blank -- with NO other line permitted. This turns the per-bullet whitelist into a whole-section whitelist and closes the free-paragraph vector entirely, independent of aggregate line count or canary wording.

issues.criterion: Scope/non-goal discipline for tools/SKILL.md's new section (non_goals[2]: 'No new capability entry for ecosystem/README.md itself'; more generally, the contract authorizes registering exactly the four shipped protocols on this surface, no more).
issues.evidence: On the same clone, appended a FIFTH bullet after the four compliant ones: '- `ecosystem/README.md` -- Overview'. Entry 13 iterates only the four EXPECTED paths and validates their own bullet's shape; it does not assert the bullet list contains ONLY those four entries. Result: entry 13 PASS with the unauthorized fifth bullet present and completely unflagged. No other verification entry (content-completeness, canary, line-cap, or any non-goal entry) inspects tools/SKILL.md for extraneous registrations -- the non-goal entries only assert byte-identity for ecosystem/*.md, schema/, and a fixed tools/ file list, none of which cover 'tools/SKILL.md contains no bullet beyond the four authorized ones'.
issues.required_fix: Add an exact-cardinality assertion alongside entry 13: count the bullet lines matching '- `ecosystem/...' (or, more precisely, '- `<path-under-ecosystem/>`') in the new section and assert the count is exactly 4, matching the expected path set with no extras (mirrors the existing exact-append discipline already applied to NIZAM.json's capabilities array (entry 5) and tools/skill.json's capabilities array (entry 9), which this document's own design_notes explicitly holds up as the precedent to follow for 'every file gets an instance-level exact check, not a vacuous membership check' -- tools/SKILL.md's routing list is the one surface where that discipline was not yet applied).

observations: ORIGINAL ROUND-0 FIXTURE RE-VERIFIED TO NOW FAIL, AS CLAIMED: reproduced this evaluator's own round-0 25-line paraphrase-mirror fixture verbatim (all four bullets stretched across 4-5 physical lines each with a parenthetical lifecycle label before the em-dash) against the new entry 13 on a fresh clone at HEAD 3aed5d1 -- `git diff --numstat` confirmed the same 25 lines added, and entry 13 correctly FAILED on all four bullets (each flagged 'bullet is not a single-line ... shape', since the fixture's continuation lines and parenthetical-before-dash format do not match the required '- `<path>` -- <label>' pattern). This closes the specific attack shape the round-0 finding demonstrated.

MINIMAL TWO-LINE STRETCH RE-VERIFIED: independently built a minimal single-bullet stretch (one otherwise-honest bullet followed by one additional non-blank, non-bullet continuation line) and confirmed entry 13 flags it via the 'continuation line' branch, not merely the shape-mismatch branch, confirming both failure paths in the check are reachable and correctly triggered, matching the generator's own dry_run_proof claim (fixture (c)).

LABEL-BUDGET SMUGGLING PROBE (quantified, judged NON-BLOCKING): constructed four maximally-compressed labels within the 6-word/40-character budget that each gloss the corresponding protocol's single most operative rule in 4-5 words / 24-30 characters (e.g. "3-verdict gate, sign-off req'd"; "one revision only, never blend"; "confidence bounded by evidence"; "every score delta traced") -- all four passed entry 13 unmodified. This is a real, quantified residual: a terse gloss of the RULE'S GIST is representable within budget. Judged non-blocking, for three reasons: (1) it is categorically smaller in information payload than the round-0/issue-1 defect -- it necessarily omits the rule's exact vocabulary, conditions, and exceptions (e.g. none of the four glosses state the exact three verdict names, the requirement that an exception be recorded in the SAME artifact as the preflight tool produced, the specific confidence-label vocabulary, or that traceability is to a NAMED finding -- a reader still cannot apply the rule from the gloss alone and must read the protocol); (2) any length-bounded natural-language field is fundamentally unable to guarantee zero semantic content -- a bare one-word label ('Preflight') and a 4-word gloss differ only in degree, not in kind, and no character/word threshold that still admits genuine one-word labels can categorically exclude all short glosses; (3) the two issues recorded above (free-paragraph smuggling and unauthorized fifth-bullet) are far more severe, cost-free, and fully-not-partially defeat the criterion, and should be fixed first. RECOMMENDATION (non-blocking, optional hardening): if the orchestrator wants to close this residual too, constrain the label to an exact enum of the four canonical lifecycle-stage names (Preflight/Baseline/Audit/Compare) rather than a free-text budget -- this fully closes the gloss vector at zero cost, since the contract's own design intent never required anything beyond those four words.

BULLET REORDERING: reordering the four bullets (Compare, Audit, Baseline, Preflight instead of the natural lifecycle order) passes entry 13 (per-bullet, order-independent) and entry 12 (membership, not sequence). This is correctly NOT flagged -- no acceptance criterion or non-goal asserts a required bullet order for this routing list (unlike NIZAM.json's key_documents array and the two capabilities arrays, which explicitly are order/append-sensitive per entries 3, 5, and 9). Not a defect.

FULL 26-ENTRY SUITE + validate.sh, RE-VERIFIED AT THE REAL, CURRENT, COMMITTED HEAD 3aed5d1 (039 fully committed -- confirmed live: NIZAM.json's top-level 'schemas' array has 14 entries including 'schema/engineering_finding.schema.json', and the 'schema' module's key_documents includes it): built a fresh, independently-authored, fully honest 3-surface edit (NIZAM.json + tools/skill.json + tools/SKILL.md, distinct wording from the generator's own fixtures) on a clean disposable clone and ran all 26 of contract 040's own verification commands verbatim, in array order: 26/26 exit 0 PASS, including the new entry 13. `bash tools/validate.sh` full sweep: 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'. This reproduces (not merely trusts) design_notes.dry_run_proof's central claim at the real, current HEAD.

ENTRY 5 (NIZAM.json STRUCTURAL SCOPE) RE-CONFIRMED SOUND AT THE NOW-REAL 039-committed HEAD: the honest edit's entry 5 passed live (not simulated) against the real post-039 NIZAM.json; separately, appending an out-of-scope entry to the now-real, HEAD-committed top-level 'schemas' array (039's surface) alongside the same compliant ecosystem edit correctly FAILS entry 5 with 'top-level key changed out of scope: schemas' -- the 039-ordering robustness finding from round 0 (previously verified only via a simulated scratch commit) is now independently reconfirmed against the actual, real commit history.

CANARY-UNIQUENESS CORRECTION VERIFIED ACCURATE: re-ran `grep -l` for each of the four canary sentences across all four ecosystem/*.md shipped protocol bodies and against tools/SKILL.md at HEAD -- each sentence is present in exactly one of the four protocol files and absent from tools/SKILL.md, matching design_notes.dry_run_proof's corrected, narrower claim ('unique among the four ecosystem/*.md protocol bodies ... absent from tools/SKILL.md' -- not the withdrawn, previously-false 'unique repository-wide' claim). The unsupported_claims finding from round 0 is genuinely resolved, not merely reworded.

SCOPE / NON_GOALS / ESTIMATED_LINES UNCHANGED MODULO RENUMBERING: diffed the revision-1 contract's 'scope', 'non_goals' (14 items, verbatim, same order), and 'estimated_lines' (95) fields against the round-0 text captured in this evaluator's own prior session -- byte-identical. The 'verification' array grew from 25 to 26 entries solely via the single new entry 13 insertion (content-completeness at old index 12 is now retained at index 12; every entry from old index 13 onward shifted down by exactly one slot with unchanged command text, confirmed by direct comparison); the pre-existing verbatim-canary and line-cap entries were only re-labeled in their acceptance_test prose ('DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH') with unchanged command bodies. evidence_convention's stated count (26) matches the live array length.

PROBE ISOLATION CONFIRMED: all probes in this round-2 review ran against disposable `cp -a` full-repository clones under the session scratchpad directory, outside the real repository tree, plus read-only `git log`/`git status`/`grep` inspection of the real repository at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework. Nothing under ecosystem/, schema/, tools/, docs/, NIZAM.json, or any .agent path other than this review's own two artifacts (.agent/qa/040-contract-review.json from round 0, .agent/qa/040-contract-review-rev1.json from this round) was created, edited, or deleted in the real repository.

final_verdict.summary: NOT approved at revision 1. Revision 1's structural per-bullet anti-paraphrase entry (13) correctly and verifiably closes the two specific attack shapes it was fixture-tested against: the original round-0 25-line multi-line bullet-continuation paraphrase-mirror now correctly fails on all four bullets, and a minimal single-bullet two-physical-line stretch also correctly fails. Both were independently re-verified by this evaluator, not merely trusted from the generator's dry_run_proof narrative. However, the fix is scoped ONLY to the four lines matching the bullet prefix and does not constrain any other content in the new Section 8: appending a single ordinary prose paragraph immediately after the four (otherwise honest, unmodified) bullets -- restating all four protocols' operative rules in ordinary sentences, at only 18 net added lines, reusing no exact canary sentence -- passes entry 13, entry 15 (canary), entry 16 (line-cap), and entry 12 (content-completeness) simultaneously. This is materially the same substantive-mirror content the round-0 review flagged, now merely repackaged outside the bullet syntax, and it is NOT closed by revision 1's fix; the acceptance criterion 'router rather than a mirror' therefore remains genuinely game-able, at a lower line cost than the original defect. A second, lower-severity gap was also found: entry 13 validates only that the four expected bullets are structurally sound, but does not assert the bullet list contains ONLY those four -- a fifth, unauthorized bullet (e.g. routing to ecosystem/README.md, which non_goals[2] explicitly forbids registering as a capability) passes entirely unflagged, unlike the exact-cardinality discipline already applied to the NIZAM.json and tools/skill.json capability arrays. A third, narrower, judged-non-blocking residual was quantified: labels compressed into 4-5 words / 24-30 characters can still convey a terse gloss of each protocol's core rule (though not its exact vocabulary, conditions, or exceptions) -- recorded as an optional hardening recommendation (enum-constrain the label to the four canonical stage names), not a blocking requirement, given its materially smaller information payload and the fact that any length-bounded natural-language field cannot categorically exclude short glosses without also excluding genuine one-word labels. Every other mechanism reviewed at round 0 (exact-append on both NIZAM.json and tools/skill.json capability arrays, single-surface-omission coverage across all three routing surfaces, C4/dangling-authoritative_source detection, 039-ordering-agnostic structural scope -- now reconfirmed against the real, committed post-039 HEAD 3aed5d1 rather than a simulation, strict version/change_log increase semantics, and whole-repository scope-guarding) remains sound, and the full 26-entry verification suite plus the full 11-check validate.sh sweep were independently reproduced as 100% passing against a genuinely honest, independently-authored 3-surface edit at the current HEAD. Required before re-approval: widen entry 13 (or add a companion entry) into a whole-section closed-set check -- every non-blank line in the new Section 8 must be either the fixed preamble, one of the four structurally-validated bullets, or blank, with no other content permitted -- and add an exact-cardinality assertion (exactly 4 ecosystem-path bullets, no more) alongside the existing per-bullet shape/label check.
